source.title: Neuroscience of apathy and anhedonia: a transdiagnostic approach

confidence_rationale: Definitive transdiagnostic synthesis establishing that an over-eager effort-cost / blunted-reward computation reproduces clinical apathy -- so the REE abandon trigger must be BOUNDED BELOW, not just above, and cannot be a bare effort-cost threshold. High source quality (NRN), mechanism explicitly framed as effort-based decision failure; transfer risk is the static-vs-recomputed-cost gap.
failure_signatures: An agent abandons attainable goals because an inflated effort-cost term makes every cost-benefit comparison fall below the act threshold, despite intact outcome valuation.
Blunted reward-anticipation drops goals at the initiation stage even when stored value is high (anticipatory, not consummatory, failure).
Disengagement generalises across goal domains simultaneously (transdiagnostic apathy) -- a shared upstream cost/value parameter, not goal-specific evidence.

mapping.source_claim_statement: Apathy and anhedonia are disorders of motivated goal-directed action -- a breakdown in effort-based decision-making for reward (ventral striatum / vmPFC-ACC / mesolimbic dopamine), where blunted reward sensitivity or inflated effort-cost weighting biases the agent away from initiating/maintaining goals, dissociable from consummatory pleasure.
mapping.ree_translation: Brackets ARC-078 C3 from the over-disengagement side: an abandon trigger keyed on a bare effort-cost (or low instantaneous value) threshold reproduces clinical apathy. The trigger must be bounded BELOW as well as above -- it cannot be 'effort got high / value got low', because that IS the apathy phenotype.
mapping.mapping_caveat: The biological effort-cost computation is value-relative and continuously recomputed against fluctuating internal state (fatigue, dopamine tone); porting it as a static threshold into an artificial trigger risks instantiating the very pathological bias the review describes. Review synthesis across heterogeneous disorders.
mapping.source_context: Transdiagnostic review (Parkinson's, depression, schizophrenia, stroke) of effort-based decision-making and its ventral-striatal / vmPFC-ACC / dopamine substrate.

confidence_components.notes: Definitive NRN transdiagnostic synthesis (very high source quality). Mapping fidelity moderate-high: the over-disengagement bracket maps cleanly; static-vs-recomputed-cost is the transfer caveat.
